message.content:
You are implementing one commit in a Rust project at /home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1. Add example values and tighten descriptions in the config schema. DO NOT commit, DO NOT push, DO NOT change version numbers or notes files. NEVER run `vc-x1 init`. Report what you changed.

# Context — read these first
- `src/config_schema.rs`: `pub struct ConfigKey { path, homes, kind, default, required, dynamic, used_by, doc }`, `pub fn schema()` (13 entries), enums `Home`/`ValueKind`, and the shared renderer `pub fn render_key_block(key: &ConfigKey) -> String` plus helpers `render_value`, `render_default_note`, `section_and_leaf`, `wrap_hash_comment`. Read the whole file.
- `src/config_cmd.rs` and `src/init.rs` both call `render_key_block` — do not change how they call it.

# Goal
Two changes, both in `src/config_schema.rs`:
1. Add a per-key **example value** for the keys that currently render a useless placeholder (`<str>`) because they have no default. Keys that already have a real default (68, 10, ".vc-x1", …) are left exactly as-is — the default *is* the example.
2. Tighten a few ambiguous descriptions by one clause.

## 1. New field `example`
Add `pub example: Option<&'static str>` to `ConfigKey` (doc-comment it: "A representative example value for keys with no default; rendered on the assignment line, marked `# example`, instead of a bare placeholder. `None` when the key has a real default (the default serves as the example)." ). The stored string is the RAW value (unquoted) — the renderer formats it per `kind` just like `render_value` does.

Every one of the 13 `ConfigKey` literals in `schema()` must gain the field. Set it as follows (ALL OTHERS get `example: None`):

| key path | example (raw value) |
|---|---|
| `default.account` | `Some("work")` |
| `repo.default` | `Some("remote")` |
| `repo.category.<cat>` | `Some("git@github.com:acme")` |
| `account.<name>.repo.default` | `Some("remote")` |
| `account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>` | `Some("git@github.com:acme")` |
| `workspace.other-repo` | `Some(".claude")` |
| everything else (incl. `default.debug`, `workspace.path`, all `bot-session.*`, `push.*`) | `None` |

## 2. Tighten these `doc` strings (leave all other docs unchanged)
- `default.account`: → `"Account profile (an [account.<name>] section) to use when --account is absent"`
- `repo.default`: → `"Repo category to use when --repo is bare (top-level shorthand)"`
- `repo.category.<cat>`: → `"Literal value for repo category <cat> — a remote URL prefix (init appends /<NAME>.git) or a local parent dir"`
- `account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>`: → `"Per-account literal value for repo category <cat> (remote URL prefix or local parent dir)"`
(Keep `account.<name>.repo.default` and `default.debug` as they are.)

## 3. render_key_block change
The block currently renders the assignment line as `<prefix><leaf> = <value>` where `<value>` comes from `render_value` (the default, or a `<str>`/`<usize>`/`<required>` placeholder). Change ONLY the assignment line:
- If `key.example` is `Some(ex)`: render `<prefix><leaf> = <formatted-ex>   # example`, where `<formatted-ex>` is `ex` formatted per `key.kind` the same way `render_value` formats a default (Str/ItemList → quoted `"work"`, Usize → bare). The trailing `   # example` marker must be present so it can't be mistaken for a default or required literal.
- If `key.example` is `None`: unchanged (current `render_value` behavior).
- Do NOT change the `#   default: …` line — a key with an example still honestly shows `#   default: (none)` above its example assignment.
- `<prefix>` stays as today: `""` for `required`, `"# "` otherwise (all example-bearing keys are non-required, so they render commented).

Factor the value-formatting so you don't duplicate the quote/bare logic — e.g. a small `fn format_value(kind, raw) -> String` used by both `render_value`'s default path and the example path.

# Tests — src/config_cmd tests / tests/cli_config.rs
- Update/extend `tests/cli_config.rs` so `cli_config_default` (or a new test) asserts, in `vc-x1 config` output: `default.account`'s block shows `# account = "work"   # example` (or the exact rendering you produce — check it) AND the tightened description text (`[account.<name>]`); and that a default-bearing key like `col-width` still renders `# col-width = 68` with NO `# example` marker.
- Keep all existing `config_schema.rs` unit tests passing (the drift tests don't touch `example`; just add the field to any ConfigKey literals they build, if any — most reference `schema()` so likely none).

# Validate (must all pass)
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
Fix until green. No `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` in non-test code.

Run `cargo run -- config --home all` and paste the FULL output in your report so I can see the examples and descriptions in context.

Report: files changed, the format_value factoring, the full `vc-x1 config --home all` output, and confirmation fmt/clippy/test are green.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s) in 0.12s
     Running `target/debug/vc-x1 config --home all`
# vc-x1 settable config keys (from vc-x1 0.71.0-8)
# Keys below are shown at their built-in default (commented
# unless required); run `vc-x1 config --validate` to check a
# config file's keys against this schema.

# ── User config: ~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml ──
[default]
# default.account — Account profile (an [account.<name>] section) to
#   use when --account is absent
#   used by: --account (init and account-aware commands)
#   default: (none)
# account = "work"   # example

# default.debug — Default --debug value when used without an argument
#   (reserved; not yet consumed)
#   used by: --debug (reserved; not yet consumed)
#   default: (none)
# debug = <str>

[repo]
# repo.default — Repo category to use when --repo is bare (top-level
#   shorthand)
#   used by: --repo (default category when --repo is bare)
#   default: (none)
# default = "remote"   # example

[repo.category]
# repo.category.<cat> — Literal value for repo category <cat> — a
#   remote URL prefix (init appends /<NAME>.git) or a local parent dir
#   used by: --repo <cat> (init remote/local resolution)
#   default: (none)
# <cat> = "git@github.com:acme"   # example

[account.<name>.repo]
# account.<name>.repo.default — Per-account default repo category
#   used by: --account <name> with --repo
#   default: (none)
# default = "remote"   # example

[account.<name>.repo.category]
# account.<name>.repo.category.<cat> — Per-account literal value for
#   repo category <cat> (remote URL prefix or local parent dir)
#   used by: --account <name> with --repo <cat>
#   default: (none)
# <cat> = "git@github.com:acme"   # example

[bot-session]
# bot-session.items — Default bot-session item set (comma-separated)
#   used by: bot-session --<item> / --no-<item> / --all / --none
#   default: "headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"
# items = "headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"

# bot-session.result-lines — Default --result-lines: max lines shown
#   per tool result (0 = unlimited)
#   used by: bot-session --result-lines
#   default: 10
# result-lines = 10

# bot-session.col-width — Default --col-width: first-column width in
#   the field-inventory views
#   used by: bot-session --col-width
#   default: 68
# col-width = 68


# ── Workspace config: <root>/.vc-config.toml ──
[bot-session]
# bot-session.items — Default bot-session item set (comma-separated)
#   used by: bot-session --<item> / --no-<item> / --all / --none
#   default: "headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"
# items = "headers,user,assistant,tool,summary"

# bot-session.result-lines — Default --result-lines: max lines shown
#   per tool result (0 = unlimited)
#   used by: bot-session --result-lines
#   default: 10
# result-lines = 10

# bot-session.col-width — Default --col-width: first-column width in
#   the field-inventory views
#   used by: bot-session --col-width
#   default: 68
# col-width = 68

[workspace]
# workspace.path — This repo's path relative to the workspace root
#   (role-specific: "/" for the work repo, "/.claude" for the bot repo)
#   used by: find_workspace_root, sync, push, validate-desc (structural; written by init)
#   default: (required; role-specific — see init)
path = <required>

# workspace.other-repo — Relative path to the counterpart repo;
#   presence signals dual-repo mode (role-specific: ".claude" for the

content.text:
Only the two intended files changed, no version bump, no notes files touched. Not committing or pushing per instructions.

## Report

Files changed:
- `src/config_schema.rs`
- `tests/cli_config.rs`

Changes in `config_schema.rs`:
1. Added `pub example: Option<&'static str>` field to `ConfigKey` with the specified doc comment, and set it on all 13 schema literals per the table (`default.account` → `"work"`, `repo.default` → `"remote"`, `repo.category.<cat>` → `"git@github.com:acme"`, `account.<name>.repo.default` → `"remote"`, `account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>` → `"git@github.com:acme"`, `workspace.other-repo` → `".claude"`; all others `None`).
2. Tightened the four `doc` strings exactly as specified (`default.account`, `repo.default`, `repo.category.<cat>`, `account.<name>.repo.category.<cat>`); left `account.<name>.repo.default` and `default.debug` untouched.
3. Factored a new `fn format_value(kind: ValueKind, raw: &str) -> String` (bare for `Usize`, `{raw:?}`-quoted for `Str`/`ItemList`) and reused it from both `render_value`'s default-formatting branch and `render_default_note`, eliminating the duplicated match.
4. `render_key_block`'s assignment-line logic now branches on `key.example`: `Some(ex)` renders `{prefix}{leaf} = {formatted-ex}   # example`; `None` falls back to the unchanged `render_value` path. The `#   default: …` line is untouched (still calls `render_default_note`, so it still shows `(none)` for example-bearing keys). `render_key_block`'s doc comment was updated to describe the new example-marker case.
5. `render_key_block`'s call signature and `config_cmd.rs`/`init.rs` call sites were not touched — the change is entirely internal to `render_key_block`'s body.

Changes in `tests/cli_config.rs`:
- Extended `cli_config_default` to assert `account = "work"   # example"` appears, the tightened `default.account` doc text appears (`Account profile (an [account.<name>] section)`), and that `col-width` still renders as `# col-width = 68` with no `# example` marker anywhere near it.

Validation: `cargo fmt` clean, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean (no warnings), `cargo test` all green (438 unit tests + all integration suites, including the 9 `cli_config` tests). No `.unwrap()`/`.expect()` introduced in non-test code.

Full `cargo run -- config --home all` output:

